title: cyclandrophora travancorica
intro: Cyclandrophora travancorica, a rare and lesser-known flowering plant species native to the Western Ghats of India, is a type of orchid that belongs to the family Orchidaceae. The plant is known to possess medicinal properties, particularly its roots, which have been used in traditional Ayurvedic medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. The health benefits of Cyclandrophora travancorica include its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help to reduce inflammation, improve immune function, and protect against oxidative stress. In traditional medicine, the plant's roots are often prepared as a decoction or powder, and used to treat a range of health conditions, with herbal preparations typically involving the use of the plant's roots, either fresh or dried, in combination with other herbs.
